speaker: Royal Advisor
text: Welcome to the Capital, {{name}}. His Majesty awaits your arrival.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: Your Majesty... the prophecy has finally begun.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: For generations, we have awaited the one destined to defeat the Dark Lord.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: That hero... is you.

speaker: Narrator
text: What kind of hero will you become?

speaker: Narrator
text: You believe every life is worth saving.

speaker: Narrator
text: You believe difficult sacrifices are necessary.

speaker: Narrator
text: Before your journey begins... tell us your name.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: His Majesty has been expecting you.

speaker: King Aldric
text: Welcome, {{name}}. The fate of our kingdom rests upon your shoulders.

speaker: Merchant
text: Fresh fruit! Fine steel! Everything a hero could need!

speaker: Captain Rowan
text: Our soldiers have been waiting to meet the Hero.

speaker: King Aldric
text: Welcome, {{name}}. The entire kingdom has awaited your arrival.

speaker: King Aldric
text: Today is a day of celebration... but destiny rarely waits for celebrations to end.

speaker: Royal Guard
text: Your Majesty! Forgive the interruption, but we've caught a thief inside the marketplace.

speaker: Narrator
text: Two guards drag a frightened child into the throne room. Clutched tightly in his hands is a single loaf of bread.

speaker: Baker
text: That boy stole from my shop! If we let him go, everyone will think stealing is acceptable!

speaker: King Aldric
text: Hero... before I pass judgment, I would hear your wisdom. What should become of this child?

speaker: King Aldric
text: Justice must be upheld. Guards, take him away.

speaker: King Aldric
text: Very well. Let compassion be our guide today. Release the child.

speaker: Narrator
text: You leave the throne room and walk through the capital. Citizens greet you with admiration.

speaker: Little Girl
text: Excuse me... have you seen my brother? He promised he'd bring bread home today.

speaker: Narrator
text: She doesn't recognize you. You remain silent as she searches the crowd.

speaker: Narrator
text: You leave the palace. The marketplace buzzes with life.

speaker: Merchant
text: Stop! Someone stole my money!

speaker: Guard
text: Mercy inspires kindness... but sometimes it also inspires confidence.

speaker: King Aldric
text: You have given your first judgment, {{name}}. Whether history remembers it as wise or foolish... only time will tell.

speaker: King Aldric
text: A Hero is not measured by the monsters they slay, but by the burdens they choose to carry.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: Your Majesty, scouts have returned from Ashwood Forest.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: Several villages report attacks during the night. Livestock has gone missing, and a number of travelers have disappeared.

speaker: King Aldric
text: {{name}}, your first mission awaits. Travel to Ashwood and discover what threatens our people.

speaker: Narrator
text: For the first time, you leave the safety of the capital. The road ahead is long, and the kingdom watches with hope as its Hero begins the journey.

speaker: Narrator
text: The towering gates of the capital slowly disappear behind you. For the first time in your life... your journey truly begins.

speaker: Narrator
text: The King's words echo in your mind... 'A Hero is not measured by the monsters they slay, but by the burdens they choose to carry.'

speaker: Old Merchant
text: Oi! Hero! Could you spare a moment for an old man?

speaker: Narrator
text: A wooden wagon sits crooked beside the road. One wheel has broken, and crates of supplies are scattered everywhere.

speaker: Old Merchant
text: If you could help me fix this wagon, I'd be forever grateful... but I know the kingdom needs its Hero.

speaker: Narrator
text: You spend the next hour repairing the wagon. The old merchant thanks you with tears in his eyes before continuing safely down the road.

speaker: Narrator
text: You apologize and continue toward Ashwood. As you walk away, you hear the old man sigh before beginning to gather his supplies alone.

speaker: Narrator
text: The deeper you travel into Ashwood Forest, the quieter the world becomes.

speaker: Narrator
text: No birds sing. No insects buzz. Only the sound of your footsteps echoes through the trees.

speaker: Narrator
text: A single crow suddenly bursts from the branches above, startling you. Your hand instinctively reaches for your sword... but nothing follows.

speaker: Narrator
text: Then you see it... smoke rising beyond the trees.

speaker: Narrator
text: You break into a run toward Ashwood Village.

speaker: Narrator
text: The village stands strangely silent. Doors are shut. Windows are boarded. The smell of smoke still hangs in the air.

speaker: Village Child
text: Y-you're... the Hero, aren't you?

speaker: Village Child
text: Please... you're too late...

speaker: Narrator
text: The child points toward the center of the village. Whatever happened here... has already begun.

speaker: Narrator
text: You follow the child through the empty streets. Every door is locked. Curtains shift as frightened villagers watch from inside.

speaker: Village Elder
text: So... the capital finally sent someone.

speaker: Villge Elder
text: We've buried seven people this month.

speaker: Village Elder
text: Every night... something comes from the forest.

speaker: Village Elder
text: We never see it clearly. We only hear screams... and by sunrise, someone is gone.

speaker: Narrator
text: The villagers avoid looking toward the forest. Even speaking of it seems to fill them with dread.

speaker: Village Child
text: My father went looking for it three nights ago...

speaker: Village Child
text: ...he never came back.

speaker: Village Elder
text: Hero... if you truly intend to help us... don't go into the forest after sunset.

speaker: Narrator
text: The sun is beginning to set.

speaker: Narrator
text: You tighten your cloak and step beyond the tree line. Behind you, the last lantern in Ashwood disappears from sight.

speaker: Narrator
text: Darkness swallows the narrow trail. Every branch seems to move, every shadow seems alive.

speaker: Narrator
text: Snap.

speaker: Narrator
text: You freeze. A twig breaks somewhere behind you.

speaker: Narrator
text: You turn instantly... but there is nothing there.

speaker: Narrator
text: Only the wind... and the distant sound of something breathing.

speaker: Narrator
text: The breathing stops.

speaker: Narrator
text: Silence.

speaker: Narrator
text: Then... a terrified scream echoes through the forest.

speaker: Narrator
text: It came from somewhere ahead.

speaker: Narrator
text: What will you do?

speaker: Narrator
text: You sprint through the forest, branches tearing at your cloak as the screams suddenly stop.

speaker: Narrator
text: The trees open into a small clearing bathed in pale moonlight.

speaker: Narrator
text: A lantern lies shattered on the ground. Its flame flickers weakly among the broken glass.

speaker: Narrator
text: The earth is torn apart. Deep claw marks carve through the soil as if something enormous had been dragged across it.

speaker: Narrator
text: No body. No attacker. Only silence.

speaker: Narrator
text: Then... you hear a weak cough behind a fallen tree.

speaker: Hunter
text: D-Don't... let it hear you...

speaker: Hunter
text: It's... still here.

speaker: Narrator
text: The hunter's trembling hand points deeper into the darkness beyond the clearing.

speaker: Narrator
text: The hunter can barely stand.

choices.text: Carry the hunter back to the village.
Leave the hunter and continue the pursuit.

speaker: Narrator
text: You tighten your grip on your sword and leave the wounded hunter behind. Every instinct tells you the creature is close.

speaker: Narrator
text: The claw marks continue through the forest until they suddenly stop, as though whatever made them simply vanished.

speaker: Narrator
text: The air grows unnaturally cold. Even the wind refuses to move.

speaker: Narrator
text: ...Crunch.

speaker: Narrator
text: A single footstep echoes behind you.

speaker: Narrator
text: You spin around instantly.

speaker: Narrator
text: Nothing.

speaker: Narrator
text: Then... two glowing eyes appear between the trees.

speaker: Narrator
text: They do not blink. They simply watch you.

speaker: Narrator
text: A deep growl rolls through the forest. Every survival instinct screams at you to move.

speaker: Narrator
text: The creature begins moving toward you.

speaker: Narrator
text: You draw your sword. The steel gleams faintly beneath the moonlight as the creature steps from the darkness.

speaker: Narrator
text: For a brief moment... you believe you can win.

speaker: Narrator
text: Then it moves.

speaker: Narrator
text: You never even see the strike. A tremendous force throws you backward into a tree.

speaker: Narrator
text: Your sword slips from your hand and disappears into the darkness.

speaker: Narrator
text: Your vision blurs. Every breath burns with pain.

speaker: Narrator
text: The creature slowly approaches.

speaker: Narrator
text: Its enormous silhouette blocks the moonlight. Two glowing eyes stare directly into yours.

speaker: Narrator
text: It raises a massive claw...

speaker: Narrator
text: ...but it doesn't strike.

speaker: Narrator
text: The creature tilts its head, studying you in complete silence.

speaker: Narrator
text: Then, without a sound, it turns away and disappears into the forest.

speaker: Narrator
text: You lie there alone, bruised and confused. If it wanted you dead... why did it spare you?

speaker: Narrator
text: You decide against entering the forest. The villagers reluctantly offer you a room for the night, though few expect anyone to sleep peacefully.

speaker: Narrator
text: As darkness blankets Ashwood, every sound feels louder than the last. Somewhere outside... something howls.

speaker: Narrator
text: A scream suddenly shatters the silence.

speaker: Narrator
text: The villagers rush to bar their doors. No one dares step outside.

speaker: Village Elder
text: Don't open the door... whatever you hear.

speaker: Narrator
text: The screams stop as suddenly as they began. The rest of the night passes in complete silence.

speaker: Narrator
text: At first light, the village slowly comes back to life. People step outside with fearful eyes.

speaker: Village Child
text: Someone's missing...

speaker: Narrator
text: You follow a crowd to the edge of the forest.

speaker: Narrator
text: The ground is scarred with enormous claw marks. A broken lantern lies nearby, its glass scattered across the earth.

speaker: Village Elder
text: It happened again... and once again, no one saw the creature clearly.

speaker: Narrator
text: As you kneel to examine the tracks, something catches your eye.

speaker: Narrator
text: Mixed with the massive footprints are ordinary human boot prints... leading deeper into the forest.

speaker: Narrator
text: Whatever happened here... someone else was present.

speaker: Narrator
text: You stand and look toward the dark forest. The mystery has only deepened.

speaker: Narrator
text: Following the trail, you leave the village behind. The trees grow older, their twisted branches blocking out the morning light.

speaker: Narrator
text: After nearly an hour of walking, the forest suddenly opens into a forgotten clearing.

speaker: Narrator
text: Ancient stone pillars stand broken and covered in vines. Moss blankets the cracked floor beneath your feet.

speaker: Narrator
text: At the center rests a weathered shrine. Time has nearly erased every carving upon it.

speaker: Narrator
text: Someone has been here recently.

speaker: Narrator
text: Fresh footprints circle the shrine, yet there is no sign of whoever left them.

speaker: Narrator
text: Your eyes fall upon strange claw marks carved deep into the stone itself.

speaker: Narrator
text: They are far older than the recent attacks.

speaker: Narrator
text: This place has known the creature for a very long time.

speaker: Narrator
text: A cold breeze passes through the clearing. Something catches your attention near the base of the shrine.

speaker: Narrator
text: Half buried beneath the fallen leaves lies an old journal.

speaker: Narrator
text: The leather cover is cracked with age. Most of the pages have rotted away.

speaker: Narrator
text: Only a handful of entries remain readable.
If only they knew what truly lives beneath these woods...

speaker: Unknown Author
text: If anyone finds this journal... do not trust the stories told by the capital.

speaker: Narrator
text: The remaining pages have been torn out.

speaker: Narrator
text: Before you can search further, you hear footsteps approaching through the trees.
You shouldn't be reading that.

speaker: Narrator
text: You turn toward the voice. A hooded figure stands at the edge of the clearing, their face hidden beneath a dark cloak.
Put the journal down.

speaker: Hero
text: Who are you?
Someone who survived.

speaker: Hero
text: Do you know what's attacking the village?
I know what the kingdom wants you to believe.

speaker: Hero
text: Answer the question.
You're asking the wrong question.
Instead of asking what is killing these people...
...ask why.

speaker: Narrator
text: The forest falls silent once again.
Tell me, Hero...
Has anyone ever asked whether the creature is hunting...
...or hiding?

speaker: Narrator
text: How do you respond?

speaker: Hero
text: You're trying to distract me from my mission.
Perhaps.
Or perhaps you're already doing exactly what someone wanted.

speaker: Hero
text: Then explain.
Not yet.
You still trust the wrong people.

speaker: Narrator
text: A sudden gust of wind sweeps through the clearing. Leaves swirl around you, forcing you to shield your eyes.

speaker: Narrator
text: When the wind settles... the hooded stranger is gone.

speaker: Narrator
text: Only the journal remains at your feet.

speaker: Narrator
text: For the first time since leaving the capital... you begin to question whether you've been told the whole truth.

speaker: Narrator
text: You make your way back toward Ashwood, the stranger's words echoing in your mind.

speaker: Narrator
text: Smoke rises from the village square. Dozens of villagers have gathered, carrying torches and weapons.

speaker: Village Elder
text: You're back.

speaker: Village Elder
text: We can't wait any longer.

speaker: Village Elder
text: Tonight... we end this.

speaker: Narrator
text: The villagers cheer, though the sound is filled more with fear than courage.

speaker: Hero
text: You're going into the forest?

speaker: Village Elder
text: No.

speaker: Village Elder
text: We're going to burn it.

speaker: Narrator
text: Several villagers begin stacking barrels of oil onto wooden carts.

speaker: Village Child
text: If we burn the forest...

speaker: Village Child
text: ...will the monster die?

speaker: Narrator
text: Nobody answers.

speaker: Narrator
text: You look toward the forest. Thousands of ancient trees stretch beyond the village.

speaker: Narrator
text: The villagers wait for your command.

speaker: Hero
text: Do it. If the creature is hiding in the forest, we end this tonight.

speaker: Narrator
text: Torches are thrown into the trees. Dry branches catch almost instantly.

speaker: Narrator
text: Within minutes, flames race through the forest. The night sky glows orange.

speaker: Narrator
text: Animals flee in every direction. Deer, wolves, rabbits, and birds all run from the inferno.

speaker: Village Elder
text: It's working! Keep the fire going!

speaker: Narrator
text: Then... a deafening roar echoes from deep within the flames.

speaker: Narrator
text: Every villager falls silent.

speaker: Hero
text: No. We don't destroy an entire forest without knowing the truth.

speaker: Village Elder
text: Then what do you suggest?

speaker: Hero
text: We find the creature... not destroy everything around it.

speaker: Narrator
text: Some villagers lower their torches. Others look at you with anger.

speaker: Village Elder
text: If more people die... their blood will be on your hands.

speaker: Narrator
text: No one cheers. No one thanks you. The crowd slowly disperses in uneasy silence.

speaker: Narrator
text: That night, sleep never comes. The village waits... uncertain of what tomorrow will bring.

speaker: Narrator
text: Just before dawn, a lone rider arrives from the capital.

speaker: Royal Messenger
text: Hero! I bring orders directly from the King.

speaker: Royal Messenger
text: You are to leave Ashwood immediately.

speaker: Hero
text: Leave? The attacks aren't over.

speaker: Royal Messenger
text: Another village has fallen.

speaker: Royal Messenger
text: The King believes these attacks are spreading.

speaker: Narrator
text: You look back at Ashwood one last time. The mystery remains unsolved, but your journey is only beginning.

speaker: Narrator
text: Three days later, the towers of the capital rise once more on the horizon.

speaker: Narrator
text: News of the attacks has spread across the kingdom. Fear now reaches even the royal walls.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: The King has been expecting you.

speaker: Narrator
text: You enter the council chamber. Maps cover the table, marked with black ink where villages have fallen silent.

speaker: King
text: Ashwood... Briar Hollow... Eastwatch.

speaker: King
text: Three villages attacked within a single month.

speaker: King
text: Whatever stalks this kingdom is growing bolder.

speaker: Royal General
text: Then we march. Burn every forest if we must.

speaker: Royal Scholar
text: Or perhaps we should understand our enemy before we destroy half the kingdom.

speaker: Narrator
text: The council erupts into argument. Every voice speaks over another.

speaker: King
text: Enough.

speaker: King
text: Hero... what do you recommend?

speaker: Narrator
text: The entire council turns toward you.

speaker: Hero
text: If these attacks continue, more lives will be lost. We cannot wait.

speaker: Royal General
text: Finally. Someone with courage.

speaker: Hero
text: We're missing too many answers. Charging into war without understanding the enemy could make things worse.

speaker: Royal Scholar
text: Wisdom before violence. An uncommon quality.

speaker: Narrator
text: Two days later, you arrive at Blackstone Fortress.

speaker: Narrator
text: The gates stand open. No guards challenge your arrival.

speaker: Hero
text: Where is everyone?

speaker: Narrator
text: The courtyard is deserted. Weapons, shields, and supplies lie abandoned where they were dropped.

speaker: Narrator
text: There are signs of panic... but almost no signs of battle.

speaker: Narrator
text: The silence is overwhelming.

speaker: Narrator
text: Then you hear it...

speaker: Narrator
text: A faint cough echoes somewhere inside the fortress.

speaker: Narrator
text: Someone is still alive.

speaker: Captain
text: ...Hero?

speaker: Narrator
text: An injured captain leans against the wall, barely conscious.

speaker: Hero
text: What happened here?

speaker: Captain
text: It wasn't one creature...

speaker: Captain
text: There were dozens...

speaker: Hero
text: Dozens?

speaker: Captain
text: No...

speaker: Captain
text: Not creatures...

speaker: Captain
text: People.

speaker: Narrator
text: Your heart sinks.

speaker: Captain
text: They fought beside the creature...

speaker: Captain
text: ...not against it.

speaker: Narrator
text: The captain grips your arm with surprising strength.

speaker: Hero
text: Who attacked this fortress?

speaker: Captain
text: They call themselves... the Ashen Order.

speaker: Hero
text: Why would anyone fight beside that creature?

speaker: Captain
text: Because they believe the kingdom is the real monster.

speaker: Captain
text: I thought they were mad... until I heard what they were saying.

speaker: Captain
text: They didn't come to conquer the fortress.

speaker: Captain
text: They came looking for something.

speaker: Hero
text: Looking for what?

speaker: Captain
text: An old relic.

speaker: Captain
text: Something hidden beneath this fortress.

speaker: Narrator
text: The captain suddenly stops speaking.

speaker: Hero
text: Captain?

speaker: Narrator
text: His eyes widen in terror as he stares past you.

speaker: Narrator
text: A blade suddenly bursts through the captain's chest.

speaker: Narrator
text: The captain collapses without another word.
He was going to say too much.

speaker: Narrator
text: A masked figure stands in the doorway, calmly pulling their blade free.

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: Hero...

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: I've been waiting for you.

speaker: Hero
text: Who are you?

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: Someone who knows how your story ends.

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: And trust me...

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: You won't like the person you become.

speaker: Narrator
text: The masked assassin slowly draws a curved blade. They don't rush toward you. They simply wait.

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: Before we cross blades... answer me one question.

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: Do you know why the King chose you?

speaker: Hero
text: Because I'm the Hero.

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: ...Is that what they told you?

speaker: Narrator
text: Without another word, the assassin attacks.

speaker: Narrator
text: Steel clashes in the silent fortress. Sparks scatter across the stone floor.

speaker: Narrator
text: After a fierce exchange, you knock the assassin's weapon from their hand.

speaker: Narrator
text: The assassin is defeated and cannot fight back.

speaker: Hero
text: I'm done killing people who can't defend themselves.

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: ...Then you're already different from the ones who came before you.

speaker: Narrator
text: The assassin smiles faintly before disappearing into the shadows of the ruined fortress.

speaker: Narrator
text: Your blade falls.

speaker: Masked Assassin
text: So... it begins...

speaker: Narrator
text: The assassin falls silent. In their hand is a sealed letter addressed not to them... but to you.

speaker: Narrator
text: The fortress grows silent once more. Your eyes fall upon the sealed letter.

speaker: Hero
text: ...My name?

speaker: Narrator
text: Your name is written across the front in elegant handwriting.

speaker: Narrator
text: The seal bears no royal crest. Instead, it shows a symbol you've never seen before—a circle split by a single vertical line.

speaker: Narrator
text: You carefully break the seal.

speaker: Unknown Writer
text: If this letter has reached you... then history is repeating itself.

speaker: Unknown Writer
text: You believe you were chosen to save the kingdom.

speaker: Unknown Writer
text: So did every Hero before you.

speaker: Hero
text: ...Every Hero?

speaker: Unknown Writer
text: There has never been only one Hero.

speaker: Unknown Writer
text: There has been one every generation.

speaker: Unknown Writer
text: And every one of them disappeared.

speaker: Narrator
text: Your hands tighten around the letter.

speaker: Unknown Writer
text: Ask yourself one question...

speaker: Unknown Writer
text: If every Hero saved the kingdom...

speaker: Unknown Writer
text: ...why does the kingdom always need another Hero?

speaker: Narrator
text: Before you can finish reading, footsteps echo through the fortress.

speaker: Royal Soldier
text: Hero! We've been looking everywhere for you!

speaker: Royal Soldier
text: The King requests your immediate return.

speaker: Narrator
text: You quietly fold the letter and slip it inside your cloak.

speaker: Narrator
text: The capital welcomes you as a hero. Crowds cheer as you pass through the city gates.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: His Majesty is meeting with the council. Until then, perhaps you'd like to see the Hall of Heroes.

speaker: Narrator
text: You enter a vast marble hall lined with towering statues. Each one honors a legendary Hero of the kingdom.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: Every generation has produced a Hero worthy of remembrance.

speaker: Narrator
text: You pause before the oldest statue. Time has worn away its face.

speaker: Hero
text: Who was this?

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: The records are... incomplete. Only the title remains: 'The First Hero.'

speaker: Narrator
text: Something feels strange. Every statue holds a sword... except the first.

speaker: Hero
text: Why is the sword missing?

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: No one knows. It was lost centuries ago.

speaker: Narrator
text: The answer comes too quickly, as though it has been repeated many times before.

speaker: Narrator
text: The Advisor walks ahead. You glance back at the First Hero's statue.

speaker: Narrator
text: You step closer. At the base of the statue, beneath centuries of dust, you notice faint scratches.

speaker: Narrator
text: Someone carved a message so small it was meant to remain hidden.

speaker: Narrator
text: 'Heroes are remembered. Truth is buried.'

speaker: Narrator
text: Moments later, the palace doors open. The King is waiting.

speaker: King
text: Welcome back, Hero. You've done well.

speaker: King
text: But our greatest challenge still lies ahead.

speaker: Narrator
text: The King's gaze lingers on you for just a moment longer than expected.

speaker: King
text: Tell me... do you still believe in the prophecy?

speaker: Hero
text: ...I'm beginning to question it.

speaker: Narrator
text: The council chamber falls completely silent.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: Your Majesty...

speaker: King
text: It's alright.

speaker: King
text: Every Hero reaches this point.

speaker: Hero
text: Every Hero?

speaker: King
text: The burden of destiny creates doubt.

speaker: King
text: That is why you must never face it alone.

speaker: Narrator
text: The King's words are reassuring... yet they sound strangely rehearsed.

speaker: King
text: There is one place you have yet to visit.

speaker: Hero
text: Where?

speaker: King
text: The Temple of Light.

speaker: King
text: There... the prophecy began.

speaker: King
text: Perhaps there... you'll find the answers you seek.

speaker: Narrator
text: For the first time, the King sends you not to fight... but to seek the truth.

speaker: Narrator
text: After several days of travel, the Temple of Light comes into view, carved into the side of a snow-covered mountain.

speaker: Narrator
text: The temple is silent. No priests greet you. No pilgrims fill its halls.

speaker: Hero
text: Is anyone here?

speaker: Narrator
text: Only your own voice answers.

speaker: Narrator
text: Sunlight pours through broken stained-glass windows, illuminating an ancient altar.

speaker: Narrator
text: Upon the altar rests a massive stone book.

speaker: Hero
text: The Book of Prophecy...

speaker: Narrator
text: The pages are carved from polished stone instead of paper.

speaker: Narrator
text: Many of the words have been deliberately chiseled away.

speaker: Narrator
text: Most of the prophecy has been erased.

speaker: Narrator
text: Only a few complete lines remain.

speaker: Ancient Text
text: 'When darkness rises, the Hero shall stand against it...'

speaker: Ancient Text
text: '...and restore balance.'

speaker: Narrator
text: The missing sections weren't worn away by time.

speaker: Narrator
text: Someone carefully carved them out using tools.

speaker: Hero
text: Someone wanted these words forgotten...

speaker: Narrator
text: As you turn another page, a loose fragment of stone falls from the binding.

speaker: Narrator
text: Unlike the other pages, this fragment still bears untouched writing.

speaker: Ancient Text
text: 'Beware the Hero who mistakes obedience for righteousness.'

speaker: Hero
text: ...What does that mean?

speaker: Narrator
text: Before you can read further, footsteps echo through the temple.
You're reading the wrong version.

speaker: Narrator
text: A hooded figure steps from the shadows between the ancient pillars.
I wondered how long it would take before someone found this place.

speaker: Hero
text: Who are you?
Someone the kingdom erased.

speaker: Narrator
text: The stranger lowers their hood.

speaker: Narrator
text: Their face is scarred, but their eyes carry a familiar determination.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: I was the Hero... before you.

speaker: Hero
text: That's impossible.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: That's exactly what they wanted you to believe.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: Tell me...

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: Did they show you the Hall of Heroes?

speaker: Hero
text: ...Yes.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: Did you notice the empty pedestal?

speaker: Narrator
text: Without another word, the stranger unwraps a weathered cloth.

speaker: Narrator
text: Inside lies an ancient sword, its hilt matching the broken statue from the Hall of Heroes.

speaker: Hero
text: That's...

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: The sword they claimed was lost.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: It was never lost.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: It was hidden.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: I don't know whether the King believes the lie...

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: ...or whether he's protecting it.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: There's a difference.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: Come with me, and I'll show you the truth. Return to the King, and you'll hear his version.

speaker: Narrator
text: You follow the Forgotten Hero through hidden tunnels beneath the temple.

speaker: Narrator
text: You leave the temple and ride back to the capital, carrying more questions than answers.

speaker: Narrator
text: Days later, fragments of evidence begin to form a larger picture.

speaker: Narrator
text: Old journals. Broken monuments. Forgotten records. None tell the whole story, but together they reveal a pattern.

speaker: Hero
text: The prophecy isn't false...

speaker: Hero
text: It's incomplete.

speaker: Narrator
text: One final document survives almost untouched.

speaker: Ancient Chronicle
text: 'When darkness rises, a Hero shall stand against it.'

speaker: Ancient Chronicle
text: 'Should the Hero seek only victory, they will become the very darkness they swore to destroy.'

speaker: Narrator
text: You read the sentence again.

speaker: Hero
text: ...That part was erased.

speaker: Narrator
text: For the first time, the prophecy no longer feels like a promise. It feels like a warning.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: Now you understand why I refused to finish the journey.

speaker: Hero
text: You abandoned your destiny?

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: No.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: I refused to become what destiny demanded.

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: Ask yourself this...

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: If every Hero eventually becomes the darkness...

speaker: Forgotten Hero
text: Then who writes the prophecy?

speaker: Narrator
text: By the time you reach Dawnfall, the city is already under siege.

speaker: Narrator
text: Smoke rises above the walls. Bells echo through every street.

speaker: Royal Soldier
text: Hero! The Ashen Order has breached the eastern gate!

speaker: Hero
text: How many attackers?

speaker: Royal Soldier
text: Enough.

speaker: Narrator
text: The city you swore to protect is falling apart before your eyes.

speaker: Narrator
text: You have only moments to act.

speaker: Narrator
text: You cannot be everywhere at once.

speaker: Narrator
text: You lead families through burning streets toward the western gate.

speaker: Narrator
text: Hundreds survive because of your actions.

speaker: Royal Soldier
text: Hero... the palace has fallen.

speaker: Narrator
text: You rally the royal guard and hold the palace gates.

speaker: Narrator
text: The royal family survives.

speaker: Royal Soldier
text: Hero... thousands of civilians were trapped inside the city.

speaker: Narrator
text: Victory doesn't feel like victory.

speaker: Narrator
text: No matter what you chose, someone paid the price.

speaker: Hero
text: There had to be another way...

speaker: Narrator
text: As dawn breaks, a messenger arrives with devastating news.

speaker: Messenger
text: The King requests your presence... immediately.

speaker: Narrator
text: The throne room is empty except for the King.

speaker: King
text: Close the doors.

speaker: Narrator
text: The great doors shut behind you. The echoes fade into silence.

speaker: King
text: Tell me...

speaker: King
text: Have you met the Forgotten Hero?

speaker: Hero
text: ...Yes.

speaker: King
text: Then you already know the prophecy was changed.

speaker: Hero
text: You knew?

speaker: King
text: Of course I knew.

speaker: King
text: My father knew.

speaker: King
text: His father knew.

speaker: King
text: Every king has known for centuries.

speaker: King
text: The complete prophecy destroys hope.

speaker: King
text: A kingdom that believes every Hero becomes the darkness would never follow one.

speaker: Hero
text: So you lied to everyone...

speaker: King
text: I gave them something to believe in.

speaker: King
text: Tell me honestly...

speaker: King
text: If the people had known the truth...

speaker: King
text: Would you ever have become the Hero?

speaker: King
text: You think the prophecy is a warning.

speaker: King
text: It isn't.

speaker: King
text: It's a cycle.

speaker: Hero
text: A cycle?

speaker: King
text: Every generation...

speaker: King
text: The Hero defeats the darkness.

speaker: King
text: Then the Hero becomes it.

speaker: King
text: The question isn't whether you'll become the villain...

speaker: King
text: The question is whether you'll choose what kind of villain you'll be.

speaker: Narrator
text: Three days later, the kingdom stands on the edge of collapse.

speaker: Narrator
text: The Ashen Order controls half the realm. Royal armies hold the other half.

speaker: Narrator
text: Fields burn. Villages stand empty. Refugees flood the roads.

speaker: Royal Advisor
text: Every day we wait, more people die.

speaker: King
text: This war must end.

speaker: Narrator
text: The kingdom waits for your command.

speaker: Hero
text: Every day this war continues, more innocent people suffer. We end it now.

speaker: Narrator
text: The largest army in the kingdom's history marches before sunrise.

speaker: Narrator
text: The battle is won...

speaker: Narrator
text: ...at a terrible cost.

speaker: Hero
text: If there's one chance to stop this bloodshed without another battle... we take it.

speaker: Narrator
text: Representatives of both sides meet beneath a white banner.

speaker: Narrator
text: The talks end in betrayal. Fighting erupts before sunset.

speaker: Narrator
text: The war continues anyway.

speaker: Narrator
text: No matter what you chose, the kingdom is forever changed.

speaker: Narrator
text: The Ashen Order is broken. Its leader has vanished.

speaker: Narrator
text: The people call you a savior.

speaker: Hero
text: ...Then why does this feel like defeat?

speaker: Narrator
text: Weeks after the war, the kingdom struggles to rebuild.

speaker: Narrator
text: The King addresses the people one final time.

speaker: King
text: This kingdom no longer needs an old king.

speaker: King
text: It needs its Hero.

speaker: Narrator
text: Thousands cheer your name.

speaker: Hero
text: I never wanted this.

speaker: King
text: Neither did I.

speaker: Narrator
text: The weight of the crown surprises you.

speaker: King
text: Rule better than I did.

speaker: Narrator
text: Five years later...
